title: ChatGPT discussion about connecting history to Project Constellation
summary: A synthetic conversation asks how ChatGPT and Codex history might become a local-only Augnes Project Constellation preview inside Cockpit.
message_excerpt_summaries: The synthetic user asks for a graph-first way to preview history as nodes, edges, tensions, and next actions.
The synthetic assistant recommends a fixture-backed ingest loop before any real import path.
Both sides keep the boundary manual, local-only, read-only, and packet-oriented.
user_intents: Preview how ChatGPT/Codex history could be normalized into SessionEpisode-like inputs.
Render a graph-first constellation in Cockpit without adding graph storage.
Copy a ChatGPT review packet and a Codex handoff packet for manual review.
product_concepts: Project Constellation nodes and typed edges
decisions: Start with synthetic public-safe fixtures before real import parsing.
Keep all packets advisory and copyable, not executable.
unresolved_tensions: Real history import needs a separate local user-provided import path.
A graph preview can clarify relationships but must not imply graph DB persistence.
next_actions: Review the synthetic ChatGPT fixture shape in Cockpit.
Decide the next local-only manual import slice after the fixture preview is understood.
